description: A Rust-based proxy/firewall for Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers

milestones.title: CLI with version and help text
milestones.description: Bootstrap the CLI using clap derive with --version, --help, and a global --config/-c flag. Create the project structure with cli/command.rs, cli/runner.rs, main.rs composition root, and integration tests via assert_cmd.
acceptance_criteria: Running with no args succeeds (exit 0)
--version prints the version from Cargo.toml
--help prints the project description ('proxy/firewall')
Unknown subcommands fail with 'unrecognized subcommand' error
--config / -c accepts a PathBuf for config file override
All quality gates pass (fmt, clippy, 100% coverage, all mutants killed)

milestones.title: Read configuration file
milestones.description: Implement the config module with GatewayConfig, McpServerEntry (Stdio/Http tagged enum), ConfigError, and the ConfigStore trait with FileConfigStore. JSON config uses 'mcpServers' key with serde tagged unions. FileConfigStore returns default config when file is missing.
acceptance_criteria: Deserialize empty JSON gives empty config
Deserialize stdio server with command/args/env
Deserialize http server with url/headers
Serialize/deserialize roundtrip preserves data
Empty args/env/headers are omitted from serialized JSON
FileConfigStore::load returns default when file not found
FileConfigStore::load returns Parse error for malformed JSON
FileConfigStore::load returns Io error for permission denied
FileConfigStore::save writes valid pretty JSON
FileConfigStore::save creates parent directories
FileConfigStore::save returns Io error for bad paths
default_config_path() returns ~/.mcp-gateway.json
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Register (add) an MCP server
milestones.description: Implement RegistryService<S: ConfigStore> with add_server(). Add Command::Add variant to CLI with name, transport type, command/url/args/env/headers. Runner dispatches parsed CLI args to the registry service. Integration tests verify end-to-end add and duplicate detection.
acceptance_criteria: add to empty config succeeds and persists to store
add duplicate name returns AlreadyExists error
Store load/save errors propagate as RegistryError::Config
CLI 'add' with stdio transport writes correct JSON to config file
CLI 'add' with http transport writes correct JSON to config file
--command is required for stdio transport
--url is required for http transport
--env KEY=VALUE and --header KEY:VALUE are parsed correctly
Duplicate add via CLI fails with 'already exists' error on stderr
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: List registered MCP servers
milestones.description: Add 'mcp-gateway list' subcommand that displays all registered servers in a tabular format showing NAME, TYPE, and TARGET (command for stdio, url for http). RegistryService gets a list_servers() method.
acceptance_criteria: list on empty config prints nothing (or a 'no servers' message)
list with servers prints tabular output with NAME, TYPE, TARGET columns
stdio servers show command as TARGET
http servers show url as TARGET
Integration test: add then list shows the added server
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Remove a registered MCP server
milestones.description: Add 'mcp-gateway remove <name>' subcommand. RegistryService gets remove_server() that returns NotFound if the name doesn't exist. Removal persists to config store.
acceptance_criteria: remove existing server succeeds and persists removal
remove nonexistent server returns NotFound error
Store errors propagate correctly
Integration test: add then remove then list shows empty
CLI remove with nonexistent name fails with 'not found' on stderr
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Full CLI lifecycle integration test
milestones.description: End-to-end integration test covering the complete add -> list -> add another -> list both -> remove first -> list remaining lifecycle. Validates that all CLI commands work together correctly with a real config file.
acceptance_criteria: Single integration test covers: add server A, list (shows A), add server B, list (shows A and B), remove A, list (shows only B)
Both stdio and http transport types are exercised
--env KEY=VALUE pairs appear in config file
--header KEY:VALUE pairs appear in config file
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: OAuth authentication - initial flow
milestones.description: Implement OAuth client for MCP servers that require authentication. Create auth/ module with OAuthClient that handles the authorization code flow, opening a browser for user consent and receiving the callback.
acceptance_criteria: OAuthClient initiates authorization code flow
Local callback server receives the auth code
Auth code is exchanged for access + refresh tokens
Tokens are stored via TokenStore trait
FileTokenStore persists tokens to disk
Error handling for network failures and invalid responses
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: OAuth re-authentication
milestones.description: Handle expired or revoked tokens by detecting 401 responses from upstream MCP servers and triggering re-authentication. The proxy should transparently retry the failed request after obtaining new tokens.
acceptance_criteria: 401 responses from upstream trigger re-auth flow
Re-auth obtains new tokens and retries the original request
Failed re-auth surfaces a clear error to the client
Re-auth does not loop infinitely on persistent failures
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: OAuth token refresh
milestones.description: Implement proactive token refresh using refresh tokens before access tokens expire. Track token expiry and refresh preemptively to avoid disrupting in-flight requests.
acceptance_criteria: Access tokens are refreshed before expiry using refresh tokens
Refresh happens proactively (before the token actually expires)
Failed refresh falls back to full re-auth
Concurrent requests share the refreshed token
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Attached mode - proxy server
milestones.description: Implement the core proxy server that listens for MCP requests from the sandboxed Claude (downstream) via HTTP Streamable transport and forwards them to upstream MCP servers via stdio or HTTP/SSE. Create ProxySession, JsonRpcHandler, StdioTransport, and HttpTransport.
acceptance_criteria: Gateway listens on a configurable port for HTTP Streamable MCP
JSON-RPC 2.0 requests are parsed and dispatched correctly
initialize, tools/list, tools/call methods are proxied
StdioTransport spawns and communicates with local MCP servers
HttpTransport connects to remote MCP servers via HTTP/SSE
Errors from upstream are forwarded as JSON-RPC error responses
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: End-to-end test with mock upstream
milestones.description: Create a mock MCP server (tiny axum app) that responds to standard MCP methods. Write E2E tests that start the gateway, connect via the mock client, and verify requests are correctly proxied to/from the mock upstream.
acceptance_criteria: Mock MCP server implements initialize, tools/list, tools/call
E2E test: client -> gateway -> mock upstream -> gateway -> client
Request/response payloads are verified end-to-end
Error scenarios are tested (upstream down, timeout, malformed response)
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Allowlist - configuration
milestones.description: Add allowlist configuration to GatewayConfig. Each MCP server entry can have an optional 'allowedTools' list. When set, only tools in the list are visible to the downstream client. Create AllowlistFilter and ToolFilter trait in filter/ module.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports optional 'allowedTools' array per server entry
AllowlistFilter implements ToolFilter trait
tools/list responses are filtered to only include allowed tools
tools/call for non-allowed tools returns an error
Empty allowlist means all tools are allowed (passthrough)
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Allowlist - CLI management
milestones.description: Add CLI commands to manage tool allowlists: add/remove tools from a server's allowlist, and display the current allowlist.
acceptance_criteria: CLI command to add tools to a server's allowlist
CLI command to remove tools from a server's allowlist
CLI command to show a server's current allowlist
Changes persist to config file
Integration tests verify CLI allowlist management
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Denylist with precedence over allowlist
milestones.description: Add denylist configuration ('deniedTools') that takes precedence over the allowlist. Create DenylistFilter and CompoundFilter<A, D> that composes allow and deny filters. A tool on the denylist is always hidden, even if it appears on the allowlist.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports optional 'deniedTools' array per server entry
DenylistFilter implements ToolFilter trait
CompoundFilter composes AllowlistFilter and DenylistFilter
Denylist takes precedence: denied tools are hidden even if allowed
CLI commands to manage denylists (add/remove/show)
Integration tests verify precedence behavior
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: CLI tools as MCP
milestones.description: Allow host CLI tools (e.g., gh, docker) to be exposed as MCP tool calls. Add a 'cliTools' config section that maps tool names to shell commands with argument templates. Create CliToolProvider that executes commands and returns results as MCP tool responses.
acceptance_criteria: Config supports 'cliTools' section with tool name -> command mapping
CLI tools appear in tools/list alongside upstream MCP tools
tools/call for CLI tools executes the configured command
Command arguments are safely templated (no shell injection)
stdout/stderr are captured and returned as tool results
Exit code is reflected in success/error response
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Unified filtering for all exposed MCPs
milestones.description: Apply allow/deny filtering uniformly across both upstream MCP tools and locally-defined CLI tools. Ensure the ToolFilter pipeline processes all tool sources consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Allowlist/denylist applies to CLI tools the same as upstream tools
tools/list merges and filters tools from all sources
tools/call routes to correct handler (upstream or CLI) after filtering
Filtering configuration is per-server for upstream, global for CLI tools
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Stress, chaos, and end-to-end tests
milestones.description: Comprehensive test suite covering load testing, timeout simulation, concurrent connections, malformed input handling, and chaos scenarios (upstream crashes, network interruptions).
acceptance_criteria: Load test: gateway handles N concurrent tool calls without degradation
Timeout test: slow upstream triggers timeout and clean error response
Crash test: upstream process crash is handled gracefully
Malformed input: invalid JSON-RPC requests return proper error codes
Concurrent connections: multiple clients can use the gateway simultaneously
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Documentation
milestones.description: Write comprehensive documentation including README with quick start guide, configuration reference, architecture overview, and examples for common use cases.
acceptance_criteria: README has project description, installation, quick start
Configuration reference documents all config fields
Architecture overview explains ports & adapters design
Examples cover: stdio server, http server, allowlist, denylist, CLI tools
CLI help text is complete and accurate for all commands
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Port and detach mode
milestones.description: Allow the gateway to run as a background daemon. 'mcp-gateway start' launches the proxy in detached mode on a configurable port, writing a PID file for lifecycle management.
acceptance_criteria: mcp-gateway start launches the proxy as a background process
Configurable port via --port flag or config
PID file is written for process identification
Start fails gracefully if port is already in use
Start fails if another instance is already running (PID check)
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Process management
milestones.description: Add process lifecycle commands: stop (graceful shutdown via PID), status (check if running), restart (stop + start). DaemonManager handles PID file operations and signal delivery.
acceptance_criteria: mcp-gateway stop sends SIGTERM and waits for graceful shutdown
mcp-gateway status reports running/stopped with PID and port
mcp-gateway restart performs stop then start
Stale PID files are detected and cleaned up
Graceful shutdown completes in-flight requests before exiting
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Logging
milestones.description: Add structured logging to the gateway. In attached mode, logs go to stderr. In daemon mode, logs are appended to a log file. Configurable log level via --log-level flag or config.
acceptance_criteria: Structured log output with timestamp, level, module, message
Configurable log level (error, warn, info, debug, trace)
Attached mode: logs to stderr
Daemon mode: logs to file (configurable path)
Request/response logging at debug level
Error conditions are logged at error/warn level
All quality gates pass

milestones.title: Reattach to running gateway
milestones.description: Allow a new CLI session to reattach to a running gateway daemon for live inspection. 'mcp-gateway attach' connects to the running instance and streams logs or allows interactive commands.
acceptance_criteria: mcp-gateway attach connects to a running daemon
Live log streaming from the running instance
Attach fails gracefully if no daemon is running
Multiple attach sessions can coexist
Detach (Ctrl+C) leaves the daemon running
All quality gates pass
